brief_summary: Two-column comparison strip contrasting coiled and straight keyboard cables across four rows: desk routing, handling noise under tension, connector drag when the board is moved, and price. Coral-highlighted boxes mark the favored side in each row; the handling-noise row is marked a tie with no highlighted box.
data_sources: keyboard-cables-compared.mdx § 'Coiled versus straight is mostly a desk decision, not a sound decision' — 'A coiled cable holds its shape, so it can run from the board to the edge of the desk without needing a cable clip or a strip of tape ... A straight cable droops unless it's managed'

keyboard-cables-compared.mdx § same section — 'A loose, properly routed cable of either kind is silent in practice' and 'the scenario where cable microphonics actually show up is a cable under tension'
keyboard-cables-compared.mdx § same section — 'the coil itself adds a small amount of drag-resistance at the connector every time the board is moved'
keyboard-cables-compared.mdx § same section — 'coiled cables cost more, almost always because of the sleeve and the coil-forming process'
